compiled_state.title: Extend WORKTREE_TRACKING resume scan in sprint and fix-bug SKILL.md with tiebreak cascade for competing branches
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Story
7de2-f557 — Competing branch tiebreak

## testing_mode
GREEN

## What
Extend the WORKTREE_TRACKING Resume Scan sub-step in sprint/SKILL.md and the Auto-Resume Detection section in fix-bug/SKILL.md (added by story 930f-8991) to handle multiple competing branches on the same ticket.

Add after the single-branch logic in each scan block:

```
**Multiple competing branches (same ticket, N>1 unmatched starts from distinct branch names):**
De-duplicate by branch name (keep most recent timestamp per branch). Apply tiebreak cascade:
1. Count verbatim task-list criterion matches: grep the ticket description for '- [ ]' and '- [x]' items; count how many appear verbatim in each branch's git diff output. Higher count wins.
2. If tied: compare test-gate-status artifact in each branch (passed > failed > absent). Winner proceeds.
3. If still tied: count merge conflicts (lower wins). Run dry-run: `git merge --no-commit --no-ff <branch>`; count conflict markers; then `git merge --abort`.
4. If still tied: most recent WORKTREE_TRACKING:start timestamp wins.
5. Merge the winner branch, discard the rest (discard = skip/log, no merge attempt).
De-duplicate multiple starts from the same branch name: treat as one candidate using most-recent timestamp.
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 9b4c-2715 structural boundary tests pass GREEN for both SKILL.md files
- [ ] Sprint and fix-bug SKILL.md resume scan sections both contain tiebreak cascade with 4 stages (criterion count, test-gate, conflict count, timestamp)
- [ ] Instructions include de-duplication of same-branch multiple starts
